name: Famous Authors
description: Novelists, poets, and the books they wrote

questions.q: Who wrote the epic novel 'War and Peace'?

source.notes: Published in serial form 1865-1869. Over 1,200 pages, 500+ characters.

questions.q: Which French author wrote 'Les Misérables'?

source.notes: Published 1862. Also wrote 'The Hunchback of Notre-Dame.'

questions.q: Who wrote the novel 'Pride and Prejudice,' published in 1813?

source.notes: Features Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy. One of the most beloved English novels.

questions.q: Who created the character Bilbo Baggins?

source.notes: Protagonist of 'The Hobbit' (1937). Frodo's uncle in 'The Lord of the Rings.'

questions.q: Which Irish writer wrote 'Ulysses,' featuring Leopold Bloom?

source.notes: Published 1922. Set over a single day — June 16, 1904 (Bloomsday).

questions.q: Which author wrote 'Animal Farm' and '1984'?

source.notes: Pen name of Eric Arthur Blair. Died 1950 shortly after 1984 was published.

questions.q: Colombian author Gabriel García Márquez wrote 'Love in the Time of...' what?

source.notes: Published 1985. Also wrote 'One Hundred Years of Solitude.' Nobel laureate 1982.

questions.q: Who wrote the science fiction classic 'Foundation' and developed the Three Laws of Robotics?

source.notes: Russian-American. Published over 500 books. Laws first appeared in 'Runaround' (1942).

questions.q: Which British children's author wrote 'Charlie and the Chocolate Factory' and 'George's Marvellous Medicine'?

source.notes: Also wrote 'Matilda,' 'James and the Giant Peach,' 'The BFG,' and adult short stories.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Adventures of Tom Sawyer' and 'The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn'?

source.notes: Pen name of Samuel Langhorne Clemens. Riverboat term meaning '2 fathoms deep.'

questions.q: Who is the author of the 'A Song of Ice and Fire' novels (basis for 'Game of Thrones')?

source.notes: First book published 1996. Seventh book still unfinished as of 2026.

questions.q: Who created detective Hercule Poirot?

source.notes: Belgian detective. First appeared in 'The Mysterious Affair at Styles' (1920).

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Great Gatsby'?

source.notes: Published 1925. Set in Long Island during the Roaring Twenties. Poor reviews initially.

questions.q: Which Russian author wrote 'Crime and Punishment'?

source.notes: Published 1866. Features the student Raskolnikov who murders a pawnbroker.

questions.q: Who wrote 'To the Lighthouse' and 'Mrs. Dalloway'?

source.notes: Modernist author. Used stream-of-consciousness technique. Died 1941.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Picture of Dorian Gray'?

source.notes: Published 1890. Irish writer's only novel. Also wrote 'The Importance of Being Earnest.'

questions.q: Who coined the phrase 'Beat Generation' and wrote 'On the Road'?

source.notes: Published 'On the Road' in 1957. Became a countercultural icon.

questions.q: Which author wrote 'The Old Man and the Sea,' winning both the Pulitzer and Nobel prizes?

source.notes: Won Pulitzer 1953, Nobel 1954. Died by suicide in Idaho, 1961.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Handmaid's Tale'?

source.notes: Canadian author. Published 1985. Sequel 'The Testaments' won the 2019 Booker Prize.

questions.q: How many books are in Douglas Adams's 'Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy' series (famously a 'trilogy')?

source.notes: 'Trilogy in five parts.' Sixth posthumous book 'And Another Thing...' by Eoin Colfer in 2009.
